title: Dual EMA Crossover Momentum
idea: Information diffuses slowly: when a fast moving average pulls above a slow one, a trend has started but is not yet fully priced in (Jegadeesh & Titman 1993, the momentum premium).
signal_rule: Hold long while EMA(20) > EMA(60); otherwise stay flat.

trend_gbm.regime: Geometric Brownian Motion, +15%/yr drift — a persistent uptrend.
trend_gbm.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe -0.60 (CAGR -8.7%) — a adverse regime for this edge; this runs against what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 20% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

mean_rev_ou.regime: Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process oscillating around 100 — pure mean reversion.
mean_rev_ou.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.27 (CAGR +2.8%) — a favourable regime for this edge; this runs against what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 40% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

regime_switch.regime: 200-bar blocks alternating trend and mean reversion — punishes regime overfitting.
regime_switch.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.69 (CAGR +9.2%) — a favourable regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 20% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

fat_tail.regime: Zero-drift Student-t(3) returns — extreme daily moves ~2–4× more often than normal.
fat_tail.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.27 (CAGR +2.6%) — a favourable regime for this edge; the thesis makes no strong prediction here. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.
